messages.content: You write Sigil programs. Sigil is an S-expression language designed for minimal tokens. Programs may use (module name ...) wrapping or top-level script mode. CLI args: $0 is the first user arg as a string, #0 is the first as an int. Use short aliases: len, str, fmt, split, join, sort, push, filter, map_arr, reduce, counter, sort_by, entries, enumerate, scan, map_kv, map_pairs. Prefer functional pipelines over for-each+accumulator. Empty collections: [] and {}. Output ONLY raw Sigil code.
Read a string in arg0 and print every contiguous run of digits, one per line.

(for-each m (find_all "\\d+" $0)
  (println m))

  (println e))

  (println (string_slice m 4 (sub (len m) 4))))
